category: decaf mentioned only in a variant name

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty labels are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster, origin, process, variety, producer, altitude, harvest, roast level, tasting notes, descriptive sensory text, producer text, and price/size/availability are explicitly present. 'USA' follows from 'Roasted in: Portland, Maine, USA' and footer text. Enum-style labels are also supported: page_type is a coffee product page; is_specialty_coffee is supported by the specialty-style single-origin product presentation with detailed origin/process/variety info; is_blend=false is supported by single-origin Rwanda with one variety; is_espresso=false is supported by 'Filter roast' and filter-brewing recommendation; is_decaf=false is supported because the main product is not labeled decaf (the only decaf mention is a separate sample variant); is_coferment_or_infused=false is supported by the standard washed-process description; price_type='one_time' is supported by the standalone product price rather than subscription pricing.

realism.reasons: This is highly plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee roaster product page. The structure is very typical: site header/nav, product title, price, size, stock status, tasting notes, roast style, origin details, purchasing controls, descriptive copy, brew guidance, related products, variant block, and footer. The voice fits the category well—concise, polished specialty-coffee language without sounding overdone. Origin/process details are coherent and realistic: Rwanda, Nyamasheke, Red Bourbon, washed process, 1,800–2,100 masl, and a washing station model all align well. The sensory notes and prose are believable for a washed Rwandan filter coffee. The harvest timing also reads plausibly. Pricing is reasonable for a small roaster. The variant data block looks especially realistic, with stable IDs, sensible SKUs, and a normal 250g-to-1kg price step. The only tiny tell is the somewhat merchandised '250g + sample' variant with package_grams still at 250g, which could be real but is slightly unusual depending on how a storefront tracks freebies. Otherwise this is indistinguishable from a real product page.

label.coffee.sensory_text: red currant acidity, mandarin sweetness, black tea structure, brown sugar, and a clean citrus finish
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Red currant • Mandarin • Black tea • Brown sugar
label.coffee.producer_text: Kanzu works with smallholder growers around Nyamasheke who deliver ripe Red Bourbon cherries throughout the harvest. After hand sorting, the coffee is depulped, fully washed, fermented in tiled tanks, and dried slowly on raised beds. The result is a precise washed Rwanda profile with clarity and a long, sweet finish.

coffee.sensory_text: This bright and polished lot comes from Kanzu Washing Station in the hills above Lake Kivu. We roasted it for filter brewing to keep the cup transparent: red currant acidity, mandarin sweetness, black tea structure, brown sugar, and a clean citrus finish.
coffee.display_tasting_notes: Red currant, Mandarin, Black tea, Brown sugar
coffee.producer_text: Kanzu works with smallholder growers around Nyamasheke who deliver ripe Red Bourbon cherries throughout the harvest. After hand sorting, the coffee is depulped, fully washed, fermented in tiled tanks, and dried slowly on raised beds. The result is a precise washed Rwanda profile with clarity and a long, sweet finish.
